All offices concerned to report/return destroyed/unserviceable property that are no longer needed to the Supply Office for proper disposal, cancellation of PARs and preparation of required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure all elected officials and heads of offices to undergo capacity-building programs on GAD. For economy, invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize the participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices, employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay officials, to harmonize GAD-related activities in the ensuing year. Such capacity building programs on GAD shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and others.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant assured to take
up the disallowance in the books of the LGU; and enforce immediate
settlement thereof.

observe the proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale
checks; (b) make the necessary adjustments on their respective records;
and (c) draw Journal Entry Voucher to take up the adjustments on the
affected balance accounts.

Accountant to strategize and strengthen its efforts for the collection of the
long outstanding receivables.

charging office expenditures from the LDRRMF. Henceforth, provide the
LDRRM Office with budget allocation from the General Fund for its
office operations.

We have commended the LGU’s responsiveness to environmental
concerns and compliance to national policies and legislation on Solid
Waste Management. We also encouraged Management to continue its
projects and programs in order to comply with the six key legal
provisions of R.A. No. 9003, which include: creation and functionality of
 the local SWM Board; mandatory segregation of waste at source;
 segregated collection; functionality of MRFs, and implementation of no
 littering and related ordinances.

Accountant to maintain special accounts for each economic
enterprise.

We have recommended that Management: (a) implement duly funded
projects within the schedule to immediately address the needs and
aspirations of the people; (b) review and re-evaluate, the unimplemented
projects whether these require additional fund, and those with project
site problem; (c) strengthen the systems of monitoring, evaluation, and
reporting development projects as basis in taking immediate appropriate
action for the timely completion of projects.

We have recommended Management to: (a) require the Municipal
Treasurer to cause the immediate return/remittance of the remaining
balance completed projects of fund transfers to the SA/to the National
Treasury and the Provincial Government as the case maybe; (b) direct
the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the debit balance of P320,172.62
and draw the necessary adjusting entries thereof, if any.

We have commended Management for its continued effort in increasing
its income thru the proper implementation of the Local Tax Code and
its Tax Ordinances.

We have recommended and Management agreed to send
collection/demand letters to persons liable and enforce full settlement of
the disallowances in accordance with COA rules, regulations and
policies on the settlement of accounts.

We have commended Management for essentially complied with the
Republic Act No. 10606 collection and remittance of contributions to
the PhilHealth.

We have commended Management’s efforts in complying with the
provisions of the GSIS Law.

We have commended Management especially the Accounting Office in
their efforts to comply with the implementing rules and regulations of
Republic Act No. 9679, on collection of employee’s contributions, loan
amortization and the timely remittance thereof together with the
employer’s share on the contributions.

We have commended Management for essentially complied with the
Republic Act No. 10606 collection and remittance of contributions to
the PhilHealth.

We have commended Management for complying with the rules set by
the Bureau of Internal Revenue which contributed additional funds to
finance the various programs and projects of the national government.

1.11 We recommended and management is amenable to:
 demand the immediate and full liquidation of the cash advances by
concerned officials and employees;
 in cases of excessive cash advance, require the accountable officer to refund
or return immediately any excess thereof;
 refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees
with unsettled accounts; and

AOs and that delay in the liquidation thereof constitutes a valid cause
for the withholding of his/her salary and the imposition of other
sanctions as provided for under the provisions of the above cited COA
Circular.

with the submission of financial reports.

perfection/issuance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

(This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspensions if
warranted); and
b. require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal
Accountant to strictly comply with the documentary requirements
stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 in relation to the

and/or monthly work schedule attendance, and leave applications
or certificate of appearance, if applicable.

from the PS, in consonance with Administrative Order No. 17.

and motivated LDRRM Officer.

9003, which include: creation and functionality of the local SWM board
and plan; mandatory segregation of waste at source; segregated
